goal: Preprocess the metabolomics expression matrix (2059 metabolites ×18 samples) by filling missing values, normalizing to relative abundance, applying log transformation (since max value283.38 >100 indicates non-log scale), and median-scaling per metabolite row, to prepare for downstream differential abundance and pathway enrichment analyses.

execution_steps.action: Load the expression matrix (2059 metabolites ×18 samples) from expression.csv, set the first column (metabolite names) as row index. Also load sampleinfo.csv to validate sample-to-group mapping (3 groups: Clostridium difficile infection [CD1-CD6], high iron diet before [FE1-FE6], Standard control [NC1-NC6]). Load metabolites.csv annotation table for reference during downstream analysis.
execution_steps.goal: Import and validate all input data; confirm sample-to-group alignment.

execution_steps.action: Identify and handle missing values (NA/NaN/zero) per row. For each metabolite row, compute the minimum positive value, then replace any missing/zero values with half of that minimum. Additionally check for any zero-value entries that may represent technical missing values.
execution_steps.goal: Impute missing or zero values using half-minimum-positive per molecule, avoiding artificial inflation of expression signals.

execution_steps.action: Perform column-sum normalization: for each sample column, divide each metabolite expression value by the total sum of that column, converting absolute intensities to relative abundances (proportions). Multiply by10^6 (scale to ppm-like values) if desired to avoid very small decimals.
execution_steps.goal: Normalize across samples to correct for total ion count / library size differences, making samples comparable.

execution_steps.action: Apply log2 transformation to the normalized relative abundance matrix. Since the maximum value in the raw data is283.38 (>100), this indicates the data is on an absolute intensity scale (not log-transformed). Log2 transformation will stabilize variance and reduce the influence of highly abundant metabolites.
execution_steps.goal: Log-transform the data to achieve approximate normal distribution, stabilize variance, and reduce skewness from high-abundance metabolites.

execution_steps.action: Perform median scaling per metabolite row: for each metabolite, subtract the row median from each value (centering), then optionally divide by the row median absolute deviation (MAD) for scaling. This standardizes each metabolite's expression profile across samples.
execution_steps.goal: Median-centre each metabolite to remove systemic row-wise biases and make metabolite profiles comparable across different abundance ranges.

execution_steps.action: Save the fully preprocessed matrix (log2-transformed, column-sum normalized, median-scaled) as CSV to the tmp/ directory with metabolite names as row index and sample IDs as column headers. Also save a short preprocessing report summarizing key parameters.
execution_steps.goal: Export the cleaned and normalized expression matrix for downstream statistical analysis (PCA, differential expression, pathway enrichment).

notes: 1) This is single-omics (metabolomics) data with2059 metabolites measured across18 biological samples in3 groups (n=6/group).2) QC samples (Fc16YH_QC01-03) are listed in sampleinfo.csv but absent from the expression matrix — they have presumably been removed pre-delivery.3) The expression data has NO missing values (NA=0, zeros=0), so the imputation step is a safeguard only.4) Max value is283.38 (>100), confirming raw data is on an absolute intensity scale and log transformation is required.5) The metabolites.csv annotation table contains KEGG IDs, HMDB IDs, PubChem IDs, and class information (super_class, class, sub_class, kegg_class, kegg_category) which can be used for pathway enrichment and metabolite set analysis downstream.6) No user-specified preprocessing exceptions were found in the research topic.7) The three experimental groups correspond to: 'Clostridium difficile infection' (CDI alone), 'high iron diet before' (high-iron diet prior to CDI), and 'Standard (control)' (healthy uninfected control).
